notes: Reverse-translating the engineering layer ('skill in action shows as the buddhi-yukta producing tight, accurate, uncompensated artifacts because their discriminating intelligence is settled; both praise-attached and fear-driven action accumulate the same binding') yields a stance fully compatible with all four traditions. Shankara's 'skilfulness as wisdom-of-equanimity-during-action' is preserved. Ramanuja's 'evenness possible through great skill' is preserved. The dual-shedding (BOTH sukṛta and duṣkṛte) is correctly preserved as a structural claim, not narrowed to one. The counter-example (novel terrain → skill ≠ speed) prevents the most common misreading. No distortion. Pass.
